title: Anu Koshal
content:
Partner, Lead, National Tax Disputes and Tax Litigation
People.ContactEmail akoshal@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Anu litigates complex, high-stakes tax disputes.
Anu Koshal is a partner and Lead of our National Tax Disputes and Tax Litigation Group. Based in Toronto, he has a national practice representing Canada’s most sophisticated corporate taxpayers in significant tax disputes and tax litigation across the country.
Anu is widely recognized as one of Canada’s leading tax litigators in numerous publications including Chambers , Lexpert , ITR World Tax, and Best Lawyers . According to Chambers , clients describe Anu as “ an amazing litigator ”, “ unbelievable ”, “ extremely impressive ”, “ a superstar in evolution ”, “ a great litigator ” with a “ steely nerve ” who “ is able to grasp issues incredibly quickly ” and has “ an incredible way of reading the room ”. Clients also note that Anu is “ personable and dedicated ”, has a “ very strong analytical and strategic legal mind ”, is “ very collected and calm” , “ good at listening ”, and is “ willing to seek out alternative perspectives ” to help them find the right solution to their dispute.
Anu’s recent and ongoing representations include:
- TD Bank in an appeal regarding the scope of the deemed trust provisions of the Income Tax Act ;
- CIBC in an appeal regarding tax credits in respect of foreign interchange services;
- Shopify Inc. in an appeal with the Minister of National Revenue regarding the disclosure of information belonging to unnamed third parties;
- Fiat Chrysler Automobiles in a claim for a $289 million bad debt deduction;
- Glencore in an appeal regarding whether GST/HST can be set-off as between commercial parties; and
- Rogers in a trial regarding the availability of tax credits relating to its goodwill credits.

#### Extensive Courtroom Experience
Anu maintains an active trial and appellate practice. In the past year alone he has represented five major public companies in contested hearings before courts across the country.  He routinely represents clients before the Tax Court of Canada, the Federal Court, the Federal Court of Appeal, the Supreme Court of Canada, as well as provincial trial and appellate courts.
Anu takes on novel, precedent-setting cases, often with a constitutional dimension. He acted as counsel for the successful applicant in Canada Without Poverty v. Attorney General of Canada , 2018 ONSC 4147, a decision that struck down as unconstitutional a provision of the Income Tax Act that prohibited charities from engaging in non-partisan political speech. The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation described this case as a “ landmark ”, and a “ David versus Goliath ” win for the client.
Anu also represented one of Canada’s largest charities in a Charter case alleging discriminatory and unequal treatment of minority charities during the audit process.  This case led to a report issued by Canada’s National Security and Intelligence Review Agency concluding that a division within CRA improperly targeted Muslim charities for audit.

#### Experience Dealing with the Tax Authorities
Anu regularly handles complex audits for leading multi-national companies with operations in Canada and has experience in a wide-range of matters including the general anti-avoidance rule, transfer pricing, financial services matters, the rules governing resource property, and indirect tax.

#### Teaching, Writing, Presenting
Anu taught trial advocacy at the University of Toronto Faculty of Law for several years and has published on various litigation related topics.  He frequently presents at leading industry conferences including to members of the Tax Executives Institute, the Canadian Tax Foundation, the Canadian Petroleum Tax Society, and others.
Anu received his law degree with honours from the University of Toronto Faculty of Law. He also holds a Ph.D. in literature and philosophy from Duke University and an M.A. from the University of Chicago. He is a member of the Canadian Tax Foundation, the Advocates Society, the Canadian Bar Association, the Ontario Bar Association, the American Bar Association, and the South Asian Bar Association.
Anu Koshal and Colton Dennis, “Alta Energy Luxembourg, the GAAR, and the Principal Purpose Test for Tax Treaties”, Corporate Finance , Volume XXV, No. 1., 2022.
Chia-yi Chua, Anu Koshal, Prince Arora, “The Impact of Expanded CRA Audit Powers on TCC Appeals,” Canadian Tax Focus , Volume 12, Number 3, August 2022.
Nicolas Cloutier and Anu Koshal, “Canada”, Getting the Deal Through – Tax Controversy 2022 , 9 th Edition.
